acceptance_targets: [chapter_12_award_01] PASS if at least one of the scene endings anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:127, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:187, or snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:299 now includes a concrete outward consequence or decision that alters the chapter's final situation; FAIL if all three scenes still end as private recognition plus departure with no new cost, contact, or obligation.

[chapter_12_craft_01] PASS if the revised span anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:149 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:177 keeps Spike's guilt legible but delivers it mostly through concrete remembered details and short flat statements, with no sentence functioning as a standalone moral maxim; FAIL if the passage still reads like Spike composing a retrospective essay about what kind of man he was.

[chapter_12_dialogue_01] PASS if the spoken beat at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:359 is no longer verbatim or near-verbatim to the style-bible example while still signaling Ed's shift into short, grammatically standard speech; FAIL if the line remains 'That was a long time ago, huh' or only swaps one or two low-signal words.

[FA-002] Pass if, in the revised passage at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:7139-7147, Ed still sounds unusually plain and exposed for Ed, the room's reaction remains earned, and the sentence no longer repeats or closely paraphrases the calibration sample. Fail if the new line is still effectively "That was a long time ago, huh" with only superficial substitution (for example, "That was a while back, huh" or equivalent).

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if at least one of the scene endings anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:127, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:187, or snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:299 now includes a concrete outward consequence or decision that alters the chapter's final situation; FAIL if all three scenes still end as private recognition plus departure with no new cost, contact, or obligation.
findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:113; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:169; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:259; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:127; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:187; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:299. The first three POV blocks each culminate in a clarified private thesis and then a departure beat, so the chapter keeps replaying the same structural move instead of compounding consequence.
findings.problem: The chapter's parallel-aftermath design is controlled, but it does not generate enough distinct outward consequence to clear award-level standards. Jet, Spike, and Faye all arrive at a sharpened private recognition and then exit on essentially the same return motion, which leaves the chapter feeling suspended rather than cumulative.
findings.rewrite_direction: Differentiate one of the middle scene turns so the chapter's end-state changes on the page. The cleanest targets are snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:113, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:169, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:259, plus the exit beats at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:127, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:187, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:299. Keep the solitude, but let one scene produce a concrete external consequence or decision that the next scene inherits instead of another quiet walk-back.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised span anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:149 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:177 keeps Spike's guilt legible but delivers it mostly through concrete remembered details and short flat statements, with no sentence functioning as a standalone moral maxim; FAIL if the passage still reads like Spike composing a retrospective essay about what kind of man he was.
findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:149; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:157; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:169; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:179. Spike's section shifts from concrete recollection into polished moral thesis statements such as the abstracted 'moral revelation' sentence and the eyesight metaphor.
findings.problem: Spike's interior narration drifts away from the style-bible brief for his private register. Instead of sparse, concrete, flat self-scrutiny, the passage gives him finished moral commentary that sounds authorially composed rather than like Spike thinking under pressure.
findings.rewrite_direction: Recast the Spike passage around the memory objects already on the page: crates, route work, the manifest note, Julia's image, smoke, and the rooftop at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:149, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:157, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:169, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:179. Let the self-indictment emerge from concrete recall and short flat admissions instead of maxims.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the spoken beat at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:359 is no longer verbatim or near-verbatim to the style-bible example while still signaling Ed's shift into short, grammatically standard speech; FAIL if the line remains 'That was a long time ago, huh' or only swaps one or two low-signal words.
findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:359. Ed says, 'That was a long time ago, huh,' which reproduces a calibration-only style-bible example line nearly verbatim.
findings.problem: The chapter copies a calibration-only example line into live dialogue. The style bible explicitly reserves example lines for register anchoring rather than reproduction, so this reads as template leakage instead of earned speech.
findings.rewrite_direction: Replace Ed's spoken beat at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:359 and, if needed, retune the setup/payoff at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:351 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:363 so the emotional drop stays brief, plain, and pressure-marked without echoing the style-bible sample.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, in the revised passage at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:7139-7147, Ed still sounds unusually plain and exposed for Ed, the room's reaction remains earned, and the sentence no longer repeats or closely paraphrases the calibration sample. Fail if the new line is still effectively "That was a long time ago, huh" with only superficial substitution (for example, "That was a while back, huh" or equivalent).
findings.problem: Ed's line here reproduces a forbidden style-bible calibration sample in near-verbatim form ("That was a long time ago, huh"). The moment otherwise works, but the copied calibration text breaks the contract against lifting example_lines and creates a process-leak inside a high-emotion beat.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the local beat at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:7139-7145. Replace the line at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:7141 with a different short utterance that preserves the intended effect: Ed dropping into unusually plain, emotionally direct syntax while looking at the record. Keep the room-stilling aftermath at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:7143-7147, but do not preserve the copied phrasing or a close paraphrase such as another simple elapsed-time remark with the same rhythm. A good local rewrite would make Ed's response specific to the record, memory gap, or elapsed childhood distance rather than to the calibration wording.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel's premise demands tonal range that most novels avoid: genuine slapstick comedy in the same book as genuine grief, syndicate violence alongside domestic warmth, existential identity crisis alongside arguments about noodles. The risk is not in the dark material — it's in the tonal juxtaposition. Commit to both registers fully. A comic scene should be actually funny, not wryly amusing. A grief scene should be actually painful, not tastefully sad. The Bebop register earns its power by refusing to choose between comedy and tragedy — they coexist in the same room, the same conversation, the same character. Do not smooth the transitions between registers. Let them be jarring when the material is jarring. Let a joke land in the middle of a painful conversation if that's what the character would do. Let a painful truth arrive during a comic scene without the comedy stopping to make room for it. The creative risk is trust: trust that the reader can hold both, trust that tonal instability is a form of emotional honesty, not a failure of control.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism is a craft failure. When a scene arrives at a moment of physical, emotional, or moral consequence, the prose must stay present — naming, rendering, inhabiting. Summary, vagueness, and decorative circumlocution at the point of impact are disallowed. If a character is hurt, drunk, broke, grieving, or afraid, render the specific texture of that experience. 'She was devastated' is not writing. 'She found his grocery list on the fridge' is writing. The Bebop world is broke, dangerous, and populated by people who have lost things they cannot get back. The prose must honor that.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters. Uncontracted forms reserved for emphasis, deliberate formality, or character-specific precision. The Bebop crew lives in contractions; full forms should feel conspicuous when they appear.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Consecutive chapters must differ in at least two of: scene count, dialogue density, pacing, primary emotional register, and structural shape. A dialogue-heavy chapter should be followed by one with more interiority or action. A multi-scene chapter should be followed by one with fewer scenes or a different temporal rhythm. Vary paragraph length distribution across chapters — some chapters should run long and flowing, others should be built from shorter, harder units. The novel's rhythm at the chapter level should feel like a jazz set: different tempos, different keys, with throughlines that connect them.
prose_style_profile.diction: Concrete, vernacular-adjacent, unsentimental. The narration should sound like an extension of the characters' world — working-class, spacefaring, slightly retro-future. Avoid elevated literary diction unless the passage specifically calls for it (rare). Avoid clinical or academic language. The narrator is not above the characters; the narrator is in the room with them. Latinate vocabulary should be rare and purposeful. Anglo-Saxon roots preferred for physical description.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, rotating by chapter or scene as indicated in the outline. The camera sits behind the POV character's eyes — we see what they see, hear their internal register in the narration, and do not access other characters' thoughts except through observed behavior. When the chapter is ensemble, the camera moves between characters within scenes but does not overlay multiple interiorities simultaneously. Maintain enough distance that the narrator's voice is present alongside the character's — this is not stream of consciousness but inhabited third person.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Jazz-inflected variation. Sentences should vary aggressively in length and structure — short declaratives next to longer compound sentences, fragments where they create rhythmic force (but never elliptical clipping). The Bebop register favors syncopation: beat expectations set up and then broken. Avoid metronomic alternation (short-long-short-long). Let passages breathe with their own rhythms. Action sequences should accelerate sentence pace. Reflective passages should slow and lengthen. Transitions between registers should be controlled, not abrupt.
